title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 with Major Coding and Vision Upgrades
summary: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 delivers significant gains in software engineering, multimodal vision, and long-running autonomous tasks compared to its predecessor.
content: Claude Opus 4.7, now generally available, is a notable improvement on Opus 4.6 with particular gains on the most difficult software engineering tasks — users report a 13% lift in coding task resolution and 98.5% accuracy on visual-acuity benchmarks. The model supports higher-resolution images up to 2,576 pixels (more than three times previous Claude models) and introduces a new 'xhigh' effort level for fine-grained control over reasoning depth. Opus 4.7 is also the first Anthropic model to ship with real-time cybersecurity safeguards, blocking prohibited requests while allowing verified security professionals access through a new Cyber Verification Program. Developers across companies like Vercel, Databricks, Ramp, and CodeRabbit report double-digit benchmark improvements, with some noting it's the first model capable of catching logical faults during the planning phase before execution begins.

title: SpaceX Secures $60 Billion Option to Acquire AI Coding Startup Cursor
summary: SpaceX has struck a landmark deal giving it the option to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion, while granting Cursor immediate access to the Colossus supercomputer powered by 200,000 Nvidia GPUs.
content: SpaceX announced it has secured the right to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion later this year, or pay $10 billion for ongoing joint development work if the acquisition does not occur. The deal gives Cursor access to SpaceX's Colossus supercomputer — described internally as equivalent to a million H100 GPUs — resolving the compute bottleneck Cursor cited as a key constraint on scaling its model training. Cursor, founded in 2022, reported $1 billion in annual recurring revenue and recently released its first agentic coding model capable of handling multi-step software tasks autonomously. The partnership follows SpaceX's February merger with Elon Musk's xAI startup in a $1.25 trillion transaction and comes as SpaceX prepares for what may be the largest IPO in history at a $1.75 trillion valuation, giving investors a concrete enterprise software story ahead of the roadshow.

title: Adobe Launches Firefly AI Assistant for Natural Language Creative Editing
summary: Adobe's new Firefly AI Assistant lets creators edit images, video, and audio using plain-language prompts across Creative Cloud apps, marking what the company calls a 'fundamental shift in how creative work is done.'
content: Adobe's Firefly AI Assistant allows users to describe desired edits in natural language — such as 'retouch this image' or 'resize this for social media' — and the AI agent automatically performs complex, multi-step workflows across Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Illustrator, and other Creative Cloud apps on the user's behalf. The assistant builds on the Project Moonlight experiment unveiled at Adobe Max 2025 and will learn users' preferences over time, including preferred tools, workflows, and aesthetic choices, to deliver more personalized and consistent results. Creators can define custom 'Creative Skills' — specific presets the assistant can execute — or choose from a library of pre-made skills at launch. Adobe is also extending these agentic capabilities to third-party platforms like Anthropic's Claude, enabling users to invoke Adobe tools outside of Creative Cloud entirely.

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4-Cyber for Vetted Cybersecurity Professionals
summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4-Cyber, a fine-tuned 'cyber-permissive' variant of its flagship model designed to lower refusal barriers for legitimate security work, available to thousands of verified defenders.
content: GPT-5.4-Cyber is purpose-built to be 'cyber-permissive,' adding capabilities not present in the standard model — including binary reverse engineering, which allows security professionals to analyze compiled software for malware and vulnerabilities without needing access to source code. Access is tiered through OpenAI's expanded Trusted Access for Cyber (TAC) program, with the highest verification level unlocking the specialized model for vetted researchers and enterprise security teams. OpenAI's Codex Security product, which launched more broadly earlier this year, has already contributed to fixes for more than 3,000 critical and high-severity vulnerabilities across the open-source ecosystem. The release arrives one week after Anthropic launched its Mythos model to a limited 40-organization pilot, and reflects a broader industry shift away from blanket capability restrictions toward identity-based access controls for sensitive AI capabilities.

title: KPMG: AI Agent Deployments Surge to 54% of Enterprises as Spending Nearly Doubles
summary: A new KPMG global survey of 2,110 business leaders finds AI agent deployment has more than tripled since early 2024 and projected AI spending has nearly doubled to an average of $207 million per year.
content: KPMG's Q1 2026 AI Quarterly Pulse Survey — spanning 20 countries — found that AI agent deployment has grown from just 12% of organizations in early 2024 to 54% today, with operations (79%) and technology (78%) departments leading the charge. U.S. organizations now project average AI spending of $207 million over the next 12 months, nearly double figures from the same period last year, while tech companies specifically project $294 million. Despite accelerating investment, 65% of leaders cite difficulty scaling use cases as the top barrier to ROI (up from 33% last quarter), and 91% say data security and risk management will influence their AI strategy in the next six months. The dominant model emerging is human-led, AI agent-enabled work: 57% of leaders expect people to manage and direct AI agents, and 87% rank upskilling existing employees — rather than hiring — as their number one workforce priority.

title: Business AI Adoption Crosses 50% for First Time as Anthropic Closes Gap with OpenAI
summary: Ramp's April AI Index shows business AI adoption reached 50.4% in March 2026 — a first — while Anthropic's market share surged to within 4.6 percentage points of OpenAI.
content: Ramp's AI Index — based on actual corporate card and invoice spend data — shows that 50.4% of businesses now pay for AI as of March 2026, up from 35% just one year ago. Anthropic made dramatic gains in a single month, growing from 24.4% to 30.6% of businesses, leaving it just 4.6 percentage points behind OpenAI's 35.2% share — down from an 11-point gap in February. Ramp's analysis found that the single strongest predictor of AI adoption is company funding type: VC-backed companies have an 80% AI adoption rate, private equity-backed firms 64%, and companies without institutional investors just 45% — a pattern that holds across every industry tracked. Anthropic already leads OpenAI among VC-backed early adopters (66% vs. 59%) and in the three highest-adoption sectors — information, finance, and professional services — suggesting broader market leadership may follow within months.
